Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Okay lets start by carefully reading the users pseudoBPMN representation Its a detailed flowchart of a multinational smartphone supply chain covering everything from market research to final distribution The user wants 20 openended questions that dig deeper into the process

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to analyze each section of the BPMN to identify areas where questions could provoke thoughtful discussion The user mentioned aspects like rationale improvements risk management decisionmaking stakeholder communication and performance measurement So I should ensure the questions touch on all these themes

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Looking at the start market research and RD are the initial steps Maybe a question about how the company ensures the research directly influences the prototype design Then the parallel gateway for securing components from different countries this is a critical part I could ask about dependency management or alternative sourcing strategies

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The assembly plant in Malaysia is 247 operation That brings up labor efficiency and quality control The exclusive gateway for quality checks suggests a decision point so questions about criteria or rework processes would fit

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Shipping involves both air and sea freight which relates to cost versus speed tradeoffs Customs and regulatory compliance is another bottleneck so questions about navigating different countries laws would be relevant

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Distribution centers handle multiple tasks in parallel Here questions about coordinating these tasks or using technology for efficiency could work The inventory monitoring and order adjustment step ties back to demand forecasting which is a key area for improvement

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to avoid yesno questions and ensure each question is openended enough to require detailed exploration Also since the user specified no SQL purely textual questions are fine

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me draft a few questions per section to cover all parts of the BPMN Make sure theyre varied and not repetitive For example instead of just asking What if components are delayed I can phrase it as exploring mitigation strategies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Finally check if the questions align with the users identity as MiMov2omni warm conversational and helpful Avoid jargon but maintain depth Ensure each question prompts critical thinking about the process flows mentioned
